component.name: ProgressBar
component.description: Displays the status of a determinate process as a horizontal fill from 0 to 1. Use when progress is measurable; for unknown duration use Spinner.
component.note: 6px tall, rounded ends. default = forest fill on a subtle track; inverse (Figma 'Transparent') = white fill on a translucent track for dark surfaces; success = completed state (forest, the system green). Synced to Figma '❖ Progress bar'.

appearances.inverse: track rgba(0,0,0,0.16); fill colors.background.neutral.default (white) — for dark/colored surfaces

tokens.motion: fill width transitions 0.2s ease; disabled under prefers-reduced-motion

useCases: Determinate task progress (processing N of M)

commonPatterns.name: Upload progress
commonPatterns.description: Bind value to a 0–1 fraction; label names the process.

commonPatterns.name: Completion
commonPatterns.description: Full bar at the end of a process.

commonPatterns.name: On a dark/brand surface
commonPatterns.description: Inverse uses a white fill so it reads on dark backgrounds.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using a progress bar when progress is unknown
antiPatterns.reason: A determinate bar implies a measurable fraction.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use a Spinner for indeterminate loading.

antiPatterns.reason: value is a 0–1 fraction; 100 clamps to full.

antiPatterns.scenario: Default (forest) progress bar on a dark/colored surface
antiPatterns.reason: The subtle track and forest fill lose contrast on dark backgrounds.
Screen readers cannot describe what is progressing.
antiPatterns.alternative: Pass a label naming the process.

interactions.motion: Width animates on change (0.2s); respects prefers-reduced-motion

accessibility.label: Provide a label as the accessible name
accessibility.contrastNote: Fill vs track is a non-text indicator (≥3:1). On dark surfaces use inverse so the white fill keeps contrast.

figmaDivergences.reason: Standard determinate-progress convention; maps cleanly to aria-valuenow.

figmaDivergences.figma: type = 'Progress bar' / 'Transparent progress bar' / 'Success progress bar'
figmaDivergences.reason: 'inverse' reads clearer than 'transparent' and matches Link's inverse naming; success shares forest because forest is the system green.

aiHints.context: Reach for ProgressBar when progress is measurable (uploads, multi-step flows, processing). Use Spinner for indeterminate loading. Use appearance='inverse' on dark surfaces and 'success'/value=1 for completion.
neverUseWhen: On a dark surface with the default appearance — use inverse
You have a 0–100 percentage — divide by 100 first
